name: api-security-best-practices
description: Implement secure API design patterns including authentication, authorization, input validation, rate limiting, and protection against common API vulnerabilities
capabilities: Guide implementation of JWT-based authentication with refresh tokens
Show how to prevent SQL injection and XSS through input validation
Demonstrate rate limiting and DDoS protection strategies
Provide security checklist based on OWASP API Top 10
Show secure error handling that prevents information leakage
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a legitimate educational skill about API security best practices. All 117 static findings are false positives: the backtick patterns are markdown code formatting, environment variable access demonstrates proper secret management, URLs are documentation links, and heuristic alerts fire because the skill comprehensively covers security topics together. No actual security risks detected.
valueStatement: Build secure APIs that protect against authentication bypass, injection attacks, and DDoS by implementing JWT authentication, input validation, rate limiting, and OWASP-recommended security patterns.
limitations: Does not provide working code that can be executed directly
Does not test or audit existing APIs for vulnerabilities
Does not generate security certificates or keys
Skill content is static reference material, not an interactive security tool

useCases.title: Secure New API Development
useCases.description: Use this skill to guide secure design when building new REST, GraphQL, or WebSocket APIs from scratch

useCases.title: Harden Existing APIs
useCases.description: Review and improve security of existing APIs by implementing authentication, rate limiting, and input validation

useCases.title: Security Audit Preparation
useCases.description: Prepare APIs for security audits by following OWASP Top 10 guidelines and implementing recommended protections

promptTemplates.title: Implement JWT Authentication
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me implement JWT authentication for my API. I need login, token verification middleware, and token refresh endpoints.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants secure auth for their API

promptTemplates.title: Prevent SQL Injection
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to prevent SQL injection in my Node.js API. Include parameterized query examples and input validation.
promptTemplates.scenario: User needs to secure database queries

promptTemplates.title: Add Rate Limiting
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement rate limiting for my Express API. I want different limits for regular users versus authentication endpoints.

promptTemplates.title: OWASP API Security Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my API against the OWASP API Security Top 10. What vulnerabilities should I check for and how do I fix them?

bestPractices: Always use HTTPS and enforce TLS for all API traffic
Validate and sanitize all user input before processing
Implement defense in depth with multiple security layers
antiPatterns: Storing sensitive data in JWT payload (not encrypted)
Exposing detailed error messages in production

faq.question: Does this skill test my API for vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides guidance and code examples for implementing security. Use dedicated security testing tools for vulnerability scanning.

faq.question: What authentication methods does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers JWT (JSON Web Tokens), OAuth 2.0 concepts, API keys, and session-based authentication with bcrypt password hashing.

faq.question: Is this skill only for Node.js APIs?
faq.answer: The examples use Node.js/Express, but security principles apply to any framework or language. Concepts are language-agnostic.

faq.question: Does this skill help with compliance?
faq.answer: It covers security controls aligned with OWASP Top 10 and common best practices, which support compliance efforts but does not guarantee compliance.

faq.question: Can I use this for GraphQL APIs?
faq.answer: Yes, the authentication and input validation concepts apply to GraphQL. Additional GraphQL-specific topics like query depth limiting are not covered.

faq.question: How often should I update my API security?
faq.answer: Review security regularly, update dependencies frequently, monitor for new vulnerabilities, and conduct periodic penetration testing.
